content.value: To introduce someone else, you can use the phrase 呢位係... (ni1 wai2 hai6...), which means 'This person is...'. This is slightly more polite than just saying 'he/she is'.

content.romanization: ni1 wai2 hai6 wong4 siu2 ze2

content.value: In formal settings, you might hear 識到你係我嘅榮幸 (sik1 dou2 nei5 hai6 ngo5 ge3 wing4 hang6), which is a very polite way to say 'It is my honor to meet you'.

content.romanization: sik1 dou2 nei5 hai6 ngo5 ge3 wing4 hang6
content.translation: It is my honor to meet you

lines.speaker: A
lines.romanization: can4 sin1 saang1, ni1 wai2 hai6 wong4 siu2 ze2.
lines.translation: Mr. Chan, this is Miss Wong.

lines.speaker: B
lines.cantonese: 黃小姐，你好。識到你係我嘅榮幸。
lines.romanization: wong4 siu2 ze2, nei5 hou2. sik1 dou2 nei5 hai6 ngo5 ge3 wing4 hang6.
lines.translation: Miss Wong, hello. It is my honor to meet you.

lines.speaker: C
lines.romanization: can4 sin1 saang1, nei5 taai3 haak3 hei3 laa3.
lines.translation: Mr. Chan, you're too kind / polite.

pages.title: Lesson 2.4 Complete!
pages.summary: You've learned how to introduce others and use formal polite language in Cantonese.
